title: China-to-Niger Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC and Power Quality — IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 Evidence for Niger PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Niger does not appear to publish a solar-inverter-specific EMC rule comparable to large-market equipment schemes. For grid-tied, mini-grid, and donor-financed PV projects, IEC 62920 for PV power conversion equipment and IEC 61000-series harmonic, flicker, immunity, and emissions evidence are the practical technical baseline. NIGELEC project engineers, ARSE review, national conformity authorities, or lenders may require model-specific EMC reports and power-quality calculations before connection or commissioning.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000-3-2 — Harmonic current emissions limits where applicable by current rating
IEC 61000-3-3 / IEC 61000-3-11 — Voltage changes, fluctuations, and flicker where applicable
IEC 61000-6 series — Generic EMC immunity and emission standards where project specifications invoke them
NIGELEC / project-owner power-quality and commissioning requirements
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence is commonly included in GB/T 37408 and related China certification files. Testing may be performed by CNAS-accredited laboratories and may reference IEC 61000-derived methods, but the report scope, limits, test voltage, configuration, and certificate language may not match IEC 62920 or the project-specific IEC 61000 clauses requested in Niger. China EMC evidence should therefore be mapped clause-by-clause to the Niger tender and NIGELEC or lender requirements.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC-related requirements
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T 37408 EMC certificates alone may not satisfy Niger project acceptance. Exporters should obtain or map IEC 62920 EMC type-test evidence, applicable IEC 61000 harmonic and flicker reports, conducted/radiated emissions and immunity reports, and power-quality studies for the exact inverter rating and grid or mini-grid configuration. Because Niger projects may operate on weak feeders or isolated hybrid networks, harmonic interaction, voltage fluctuation, and inverter stacking behaviour should be checked under local operating assumptions.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but commonly mandatory in practice where NIGELEC, national conformity authorities, or donor/lender specifications require IEC EMC and power-quality evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T EMC evidence is a China-side technical baseline; it is not automatically accepted as IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 evidence in Niger

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Map China EMC files to IEC 62920 and the exact IEC 61000 clauses named by the Niger project. For weak-grid, mini-grid, or donor-financed systems, include power-quality analysis and local operating assumptions rather than relying only on a China GB/T EMC certificate.

rows.requirement: NIGELEC Grid Connection — 50 Hz / 220 V / 380 V Settings, Anti-Islanding, and Project Approval
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied PV inverters in Niger should be treated as requiring NIGELEC technical connection review and, where generation or electricity sale is regulated, ARSE authorisation or licensing. Niger is a 50 Hz market and the commonly stated low-voltage supply is 220/380 V, so inverter firmware, protection thresholds, reconnection delay, voltage/frequency ride-through behaviour, and anti-islanding functions must be validated against Niger project settings rather than assumed from China factory defaults. In the absence of a publicly confirmed Niger inverter grid code, IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 are the practical evidence set normally requested by engineers, utilities, and donor-financed projects.
target_standards: NIGELEC technical connection review for grid-tied projects — project-specific gate
ARSE authorisation or licensing for regulated electricity activities — Niger energy-sector gate
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Niger low-voltage grid context commonly stated as 220/380 V, 50 Hz
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power-station connection, NB/T 32004 for grid-connected PV inverter technical specifications, and GB/T 37408 for PV grid-connected inverter requirements. These documents are prepared for China grid operators and China conformity programmes. Because Niger approval is controlled by local national conformity, ARSE/NIGELEC project review, and lender specifications, Chinese grid-connection approvals do not create automatic acceptance in Niger even though both markets use 50 Hz and nominal 220/380 V low-voltage settings.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China GB/T grid evidence is not a substitute for NIGELEC connection approval or ARSE treatment in Niger. Exporters should prepare a Niger-specific grid-settings file, IEC 62116 anti-islanding report, IEC 61727 utility-interface report, protection-setting schedule, single-line diagram, commissioning test procedure, and evidence that the supplied firmware profile matches the NIGELEC-approved settings. For utility-scale solar and donor-financed projects, confirm any tender-specific grid-code, SCADA, reactive-power, fault-ride-through, and power-quality requirements before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for grid-tied projects: NIGELEC connection acceptance and any applicable ARSE authorisation/licence are project gates; IEC evidence is normally required by project engineers or lenders where local rules reference IEC
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China-side grid and certification expectations only; it is not transferable to Niger without local review

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat NIGELEC connection acceptance and ARSE treatment as separate Niger project gates. Do not rely on China GB/T grid certificates alone. Prepare IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence, a Niger-specific firmware/protection profile, and commissioning records for the exact inverter model before committing the equipment to a grid-tied Niger project.

rows.requirement: Off-Grid and Mini-Grid Solar — IEC TS 62257 / VeraSol Evidence, ARSE Treatment, and Donor Specifications
rows.target_requirement: Niger has a strong off-grid and mini-grid solar use case because electrification is low and many rural loads are far from the NIGELEC network. For isolated PV inverter systems and mini-grids, the practical acceptance package is often driven by project documents from the government, ARSE, rural electrification agencies, World Bank, AfDB, IFC, or other donors. IEC TS 62257 is the core international reference for small renewable-energy and hybrid rural electrification systems, and VeraSol quality-assurance requirements may be specified for off-grid appliances or solar-home-system equipment. Inverters must also be sized and derated for Sahel heat, dust, weak maintenance conditions, and battery/hybrid operation.
target_standards: IEC TS 62257 series — Recommendations for renewable energy and hybrid systems for rural electrification
VeraSol quality assurance framework — commonly referenced for off-grid solar product quality where project specifications require it
ARSE and Niger rural-energy project approval or licensing requirements where mini-grid electricity service is regulated
World Bank / AfDB / IFC project specifications for Niger solar, mini-grid, or energy-access programmes where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China-side inverter evidence is usually centred on grid-connected PV standards such as GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, and GB/T 19964. These are not designed as a complete off-grid rural electrification acceptance package. Chinese factory test reports may demonstrate inverter efficiency, battery compatibility, and environmental operation, but Niger off-grid projects may require IEC TS 62257-aligned system design evidence, donor quality-assurance documentation, field-service plans, spare-parts availability, and operation in high ambient temperature and dust.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
rows.gap: Gap: A China grid-connected inverter certificate does not prove suitability for Niger off-grid or mini-grid service. Exporters should prepare IEC TS 62257-oriented system documentation, battery and hybrid-controller compatibility evidence, islanded-mode protection settings, black-start and load-step test evidence, dust/heat derating curves, service and spare-parts plans, and any VeraSol or donor-specific quality documentation required by the tender. Confirm ARSE and local concession or licence treatment before offering electricity service through a mini-grid.

mandatory_status.target: Project-dependent but often mandatory in practice for donor-financed off-grid or mini-grid projects; ARSE or concession treatment may be mandatory where electricity service is regulated
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T grid-connected inverter documentation is not an off-grid or mini-grid rural electrification approval package

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For Niger off-grid and mini-grid projects, treat IEC TS 62257-style system evidence, donor quality requirements, field-service capacity, and ARSE/concession treatment as separate from China grid-inverter certification. Confirm VeraSol or other quality-assurance clauses in the tender before selecting the inverter family.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — Niger National Conformity, ARSE/NIGELEC, and Lender Review
rows.target_requirement: For PV inverters used in Niger, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the defensible safety evidence baseline for national conformity review, ARSE/NIGELEC project review, and donor/lender procurement. Niger standardisation practice is understood to adopt or reference international IEC standards where national solar-inverter rules are not separately published. Safety evidence should cover the exact inverter model or certified family, installation altitude and ambient temperature range, protective earthing, DC isolation, thermal protection, enclosure ingress protection, and operation in high heat and dust typical of Sahel sites.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
Niger national conformity and standards-adoption review where applicable
ARSE / NIGELEC / project-owner technical documentation requirements where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China-side PV inverter safety files commonly reference GB/T 37408 and domestic CQC/CGC or laboratory reports. These documents may demonstrate useful design and production controls, but they are not automatically equivalent to IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates for Niger projects. Where Chinese reports are not issued against IEC 62109 by an internationally recognised laboratory, they should be treated as supporting evidence only.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
CQC / CGC voluntary PV inverter certification schemes where used by the manufacturer
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T safety evidence does not remove the need for IEC 62109-1/-2 model coverage where Niger authorities, NIGELEC, ARSE, project owners, or lenders request IEC evidence. Exporters should provide IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates and reports, CB Test Certificate or ILAC-accredited laboratory evidence where available, model-family mapping, bill-of-materials change control, installation manual in French where required, and derating curves for operation at 45 C or higher with dust-prone outdoor enclosures.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Niger national conformity, NIGELEC connection review, ARSE authorisation, project-owner, or donor/lender specifications require IEC inverter safety evidence; otherwise still a practical baseline for bankable PV procurement
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 37408 and domestic voluntary certification are China-side evidence; not automatically equivalent to IEC 62109 acceptance in Niger

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Provide IEC 62109-1/-2 safety evidence for the exact inverter model or certified family before Niger submission. China GB/T 37408 safety files can support the dossier but should not be presented as a substitute for IEC 62109 where NIGELEC, ARSE, national conformity authorities, or lenders request IEC evidence.
